content: If we map Self-Refine’s task-specific iterative self-feedback loop onto Reflexion’s dynamic long-term memory, we can build a hierarchical self-improving agent that refines both its immediate outputs and its accumulated memory of past problem-solving trajectories over time. This hybrid design retains the key advantage of neither requiring extra supervised training nor external reward signals, while fixing the limitation of existing frameworks that only refine single outputs without improving the foundational knowledge an agent draws on for future tasks.
